sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: HP Inc’s Stock Under the Microscope: Can HPQ Turn a Tepid Rally Into a Real Breakout?
feed.summary: HP Inc's stock (HPQ) has seen a cautious rally recently, with a 25-30% gain over the last year, driven by improving PC demand and AI ambitions. While analysts lean neutral to moderately positive, the stock remains below its 52-week high, suggesting potential for further upside if execution and macro conditions align. The company's strategy focuses on recurring revenue from services and AI-enabled devices, positioning it as a steady investment rather than a high-growth momentum play.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts See Cipher Mining (CIFR) as a Buy: Should You Invest?
feed.summary: Cipher Mining (CIFR) currently holds an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.29, indicating a consensus between Strong Buy and Buy among 14 brokerage firms. However, the article advises caution against making investment decisions solely based on these recommendations due to potential positive bias from brokerage firms' vested interests. It recommends using the Zacks Rank, a proprietary stock rating tool based on earnings estimate revisions, as a more reliable indicator for near-term price performance and suggests validating ABR with Zacks Rank for better investment decisions.

feed.title: Century Communities (CCS) Projects Strong Home Deliveries for 2026
feed.summary: Century Communities (CCS) projects significant growth in home deliveries and revenue for 2026, anticipating 10,000 to 11,000 new home deliveries and revenues between $3.6 billion and $4.1 billion. The company, which focuses on single-family homes across multiple U.S. regions, exhibits strong financial health with a balanced approach to debt and stable profitability. While CCS shows strong liquidity and an Altman Z-Score indicating good financial health, its ROIC is currently below its WACC, and the real estate sector's cyclical risks suggest investors should consider volatility.

feed.title: Assurant Appoints Lynn Blake to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. announced the appointment of Lynn Blake, former Executive Vice President and Chief Investment Officer at State Street Investment Management, to its Board of Directors, effective January 28, 2026. Blake will serve on the company’s Finance and Risk Committee and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee, bringing over 30 years of experience in asset management, investment strategies, and risk management. Additionally, Debra Perry plans to retire from the Assurant Board of Directors after the 2026 annual shareholder meeting.

feed.title: Equifax launches employment insights solutions for auto dealers
feed.summary: Equifax has introduced two new Employment Insights solutions for auto dealers to provide verified employment and income data during the car-buying process. These solutions integrate data from The Work Number with Equifax’s Consumer Credit Report, aiding in prequalification and financing stages to help dealers assess purchasing power and reduce buyback risk. The new offerings aim to streamline the loan approval process by moving beyond self-reported consumer information.

feed.title: United rentals posts record Q4 revenue, plans $1.5 billion share buyback
feed.summary: United Rentals (NYSE:URI) announced record Q4 2025 revenue of $4.21 billion and plans for a $1.5 billion share repurchase in 2026, alongside a new $5 billion repurchase program. Despite a 5.2% dip in net income for the quarter, the company significantly increased its quarterly dividend by 10% and reported strong operating and free cash flow for the full year. Specialty rentals showed robust growth, and the company forecasts further revenue and adjusted EBITDA growth for 2026.

feed.title: STERIS plc announces $0.63 quarterly dividend payable in March
feed.summary: STERIS plc announced a quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share, payable on March 19, 2026, continuing its 21-year streak of dividend payments. The company, which specializes in infection prevention solutions, boasts a "GREAT" financial health score from InvestingPro and an annual yield of 0.96%. This dividend announcement follows the company's Q2 fiscal year 2025 earnings report where it surpassed EPS expectations but missed revenue projections.

feed.title: Archer Daniels Midland hit with $40M fine in price-fixing probe, 30 years after fraud scandal
feed.summary: Archer Daniels Midland (ADM) has been fined $40 million by the SEC for allegedly misleading investors about the profits of its nutrition segment. This settlement comes over 30 years after a prior price-fixing scandal involving the company. While ADM did not admit or deny wrongdoing, former executives Vince Macciocchi and Ray Young also face civil penalties, and former CFO Vikram Luthar is charged with accounting fraud.

feed.title: Massachusetts Home Depot closed due to "significant snow" on roof, customers scramble for supplies
feed.summary: A Home Depot in Watertown, Massachusetts, was forced to close due to a "significant snow" accumulation on its roof following a recent storm, disrupting daily life in Greater Boston. This closure left many customers, including electricians and residents needing snowblowers, scrambling for essential supplies. While city crews work on cleanup, customers are driving to other locations, with one local expressing optimism about New England's winter weather despite the inconvenience.

feed.title: Fair Isaac turns in Q1 profit beat as scores revenues shine; repeats guide (FICO:NYSE)
feed.summary: Fair Isaac (FICO) reported a beat on its Q1 fiscal 2026 profit and revenue estimates, driven by strong performance in its scores segment. Despite the positive results, the company's shares dipped 2% in after-hours trading, as it reiterated its full-year 2026 guidance for adjusted EPS and revenues, which were below consensus estimates.

feed.title: (WM) Waste Management Expects 2026 Revenue Range $26.43B-$26.63B, vs. FactSet Est of $26.59B
feed.summary: Waste Management Inc. (WM) expects its 2026 revenue to be in the range of $26.43 billion to $26.63 billion. This forecast is in line with FactSet's estimated revenue of $26.59 billion for the same period. The announcement also included Q4 adjusted EPS of $1.93 per share against an estimated $1.95, and Q4 revenue of $6.31 billion against an estimated $6.39 billion.

feed.title: MSCI, BlackRock extend long-term equity index licensing pact
feed.summary: MSCI and BlackRock have extended their ETF index licensing agreement until March 31, 2035, with automatic renewals. The revised deal involves adjusted fee schedules for certain funds based on assets under management and expense ratios, aiming to support ETF growth without significantly altering the overall fee structure. BlackRock's significant ownership of MSCI shares further highlights the strong commercial and investment relationship between the two companies.

feed.title: United Rentals Posts Record 2025 Results, Sets 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: United Rentals (URI) reported record fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, with total revenue of $4.21 billion and rental revenue of $3.58 billion, despite margin pressures. The company also announced a positive 2026 outlook, projecting total revenue of $16.8–$17.3 billion and free cash flow of $2.15–$2.45 billion, along with a new $5 billion share repurchase authorization and a 10% dividend increase. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates URI as "Outperform," citing strong financial performance but noting risks like high debt and margin pressures.

feed.title: EXEC: VF Corp. Shares Tumble as Weak Fiscal Q1 Guidance Overshadows Q4 Earnings Beat
feed.summary: VF Corporation's shares fell after the company released weak fiscal Q1 guidance, despite reporting better-than-expected Q4 earnings driven by The North Face and Timberland. The Vans brand continued its sales decline, although the CEO expressed optimism about its turnaround, noting growth in e-commerce and positive responses to new products. Overall, the company forecasts flat to slightly up revenue for the fiscal year, with improved gross and operating margins.

feed.title: Chain Bridge Bancorp (NYSE:CBNA) Issues Earnings Results
feed.summary: Chain Bridge Bancorp (NYSE:CBNA) reported Q4 earnings of $0.81 EPS, exceeding the consensus estimate of $0.79. Despite beating earnings expectations, the stock traded down 2.7% to $32.79. Analyst ratings for CBNA include "Reduce" with a $33.00 target price, and institutional investors have recently adjusted their positions in the company.

feed.title: Home Depot cuts 800 jobs
feed.summary: The Home Depot has eliminated approximately 800 roles at its Atlanta store support center, primarily impacting its technology organization. This move coincides with the retailer mandating a five-day-a-week return-to-office policy for corporate staff, aiming to simplify operations and increase agility. The job cuts follow a third quarter where Home Depot missed profit expectations and are part of a broader trend of layoffs in the retail industry.

feed.title: NVIDIA Sets Conference Call for Fourth-Quarter Financial Results
feed.summary: NVIDIA announced it will host a conference call on Wednesday, February 25, at 2 p.m. PT to discuss its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2026, which ended January 25, 2026. Prior to the call, written commentary from CFO Colette Kress will be posted on investor.nvidia.com after the results are publicly announced. The webcast will be available for replay until the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 earnings call.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson beats profit outlook, misses on revenue due to lower pricing, ocean volume slump (CHRW:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) exceeded its profit expectations in the fourth quarter despite facing "significant market headwinds," leading to a rise in after-hours trading. However, the company missed its revenue targets due to lower pricing and a significant slump in ocean freight volumes. The challenging macro environment impacted global freight demand and increased spot costs.

feed.title: AIP Corp, Caterpillar form alliance to deploy 2 GW power for AI data centers
feed.summary: American Intelligence & Power Corporation (AIP Corp) has partnered with Caterpillar Inc. and Boyd CAT to deploy 2 gigawatts of natural gas generator sets for its Monarch Compute Campus in West Virginia, specifically to power AI data centers. This strategic alliance includes a purchase agreement for generators with advanced emissions controls, integrated with battery energy storage systems, to address the variable load demands of AI operations. Caterpillar's involvement is supported by its strong financial performance and its ability to provide vendor equipment financing for the project.

feed.title: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4
feed.summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) reported better-than-expected revenue in Q4 CY2025, with sales up 22.1% year-on-year to $5.34 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. The company also provided surprisingly strong revenue guidance for Q1 CY2026, projected at $5.7 billion at the midpoint, exceeding analyst expectations. This performance, along with solid adjusted EPS and operating income, caps a record year for the semiconductor equipment maker, demonstrating sustained growth and effective inventory management.

feed.title: IBM (NYSE:IBM) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q4 CY2025, Stock Soars
feed.summary: IBM reported strong Q4 CY2025 results, significantly beating Wall Street's revenue and adjusted EPS expectations, with sales up 12.1% year-on-year to $19.69 billion. The company's performance was driven by double-digit growth in Software and Infrastructure, alongside a generative AI book of business exceeding $12.5 billion. IBM's stock surged 7.9% post-announcement, and the company issued optimistic guidance for 2026, forecasting over 5% constant currency revenue growth.

feed.title: WM Announces Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Earnings
feed.summary: WM announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2025, driven by operational excellence and strategic investments. The company delivered record performance in operating expenses as a percentage of revenue and saw significant growth in cash flow from operations and adjusted operating EBITDA. WM expects continued growth in 2026, including a nearly 30% increase in free cash flow, and plans to return approximately $3.5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

feed.title: Southwest: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV) reported a fourth-quarter profit of $323 million, or 61 cents per share, which adjusted to 58 cents per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. However, the airline's revenue of $7.44 billion fell short of analysts' forecasts. Southwest anticipates earnings of 45 cents per share for the current quarter and $4 per share for the full year.

feed.title: Shift5 hires former L3Harris exec as interim CEO
feed.summary: Shift5, a cybersecurity startup, has appointed Toby Magsig, formerly of L3Harris Technologies, as its interim chief executive. Magsig takes over from co-founder Josh Lospinoso, who will transition to focus on long-term planning while remaining on the board of directors. Shift5, known for its operational technology products for defense and transportation, recently secured a $75 million Series C funding round.

feed.title: Axon Enterprise Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Axon Enterprise Inc. (AXON) stock fell on Wednesday, underperforming competitors such as Motorola Solutions and Vuzix Corp. The stock closed at $247.90, representing a 0.23% decline, while the S&P 500 rose 0.82%.

feed.title: Teva Pharma stock price target raised to $37 from $36 at BofA Securities
feed.summary: BofA Securities has increased its price target for Teva Pharmaceutical Industries (NYSE:TEVA) to $37.00 from $36.00, reiterating a Buy rating based on a "solid" fourth-quarter update. The firm cited strong brand business performance and encouraging fiscal year 2026 guidance, which includes mid-single-digit revenue growth and confidence in managing the loss of exclusivity for generic Revlimid. Despite the stock appearing slightly overvalued by some metrics, net income is expected to grow, and Teva recently surpassed analyst expectations for its Q4 2025 earnings and revenue.

feed.title: IBM Stock Surges As Q4 Earnings Top Estimates: 'We Enter 2026 With Momentum'
feed.summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter, exceeding revenue and earnings estimates. The company saw significant growth in Software, Consulting, and Infrastructure segments, fueled by its generative AI business. IBM also delivered positive full-year 2026 expectations, anticipating revenue of $70.91 billion and increased free cash flow, leading to a surge in its stock price.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) reported quarterly earnings, beating EPS estimates with $0.58, though revenue of $7.44 billion missed consensus. The airline announced a quarterly dividend of $0.18, resulting in a high payout ratio of 110.77%, which could raise sustainability concerns. The stock currently has a "Hold" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $42.37.

feed.title: HC rejects P&G’s exclusive claim over the term ‘vapo’
feed.summary: The Madras High Court rejected Procter & Gamble's claim of exclusive rights over the term 'vapo,' stating it is merely an abbreviation of 'vapour' and is descriptive and common to the trade. Justice N Senthilkumar observed that 'vapo' is publici juris, meaning it belongs to the public, and therefore, P&G cannot claim an exclusive right to its use. The court also found that the trademarks 'Vicks VapoRub' and IPI India's 'Vaporin Cold Rub' and 'Vaporin' are distinct and not deceptively similar, both visually and phonetically.

feed.title: Lam Research Beats Q2 Estimates, Strong Q3 Guidance
feed.summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ: LRCX) reported strong second-quarter earnings, surpassing analyst estimates for both earnings per share and revenue. The company also provided robust third-quarter guidance, expecting higher adjusted EPS and revenue than current analyst projections. This positive performance led to a slight increase in the company's stock during extended trading.

feed.title: Century Communities (NYSE:CCS) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.20 EPS
feed.summary: Century Communities (NYSE:CCS) reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates by $0.20 with an EPS of $1.59. The construction company's stock traded up on the news, demonstrating solid financial metrics including a 5.16% net margin and 9.36% ROE. Despite mixed analyst ratings, the company also recently declared a quarterly dividend of $0.29 per share.

feed.title: Bit Digital Reaffirms Long-Term Investment in WhiteFiber Shares
feed.summary: Bit Digital, Inc. has reaffirmed its long-term commitment to WhiteFiber, Inc., announcing that it will not sell its approximately 27 million shares in WhiteFiber in 2026, even after the IPO lockup period expires on February 2, 2026. The company views WhiteFiber as a core strategic holding, central to its AI infrastructure exposure alongside its Ethereum-focused digital asset platform. This decision reflects Bit Digital's confidence in WhiteFiber's long-term growth and aligns with its strategic investment approach.

feed.title: STERIS Announces Dividend of $0.63 per share
feed.summary: STERIS plc (NYSE: STE) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share. This dividend is scheduled for payment on March 19, 2026, to shareholders who are on record by the close of business on February 17, 2026. STERIS specializes in providing products and services focused on infection prevention within patient care.

feed.title: Athene Holding Ltd. Declares First Quarter 2026 Preferred Stock Dividends
feed.summary: Athene Holding Ltd. has declared its first-quarter 2026 preferred stock dividends for its Series A, B, D, and E non-cumulative preferred shares. The dividends are payable on March 30, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2026. Each series has a specific per-share dividend amount, with corresponding payments for depositary shares.

feed.title: Home Depot to cut 800 corporate jobs at its Atlanta store support center
feed.summary: Home Depot announced it is cutting 800 corporate jobs at its Atlanta store support center and requiring corporate employees to return to the office five days a week. The company cited a goal to increase agility and better connect with frontline associates. This move comes as U.S. housing demand is challenged by rising unemployment and high home prices, impacting the home improvement giant's financial projections.

feed.title: Why Las Vegas Sands Stock Is Falling After Q4 Double Beat
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (NYSE: LVS) reported a "double beat" for its fourth-quarter financial results, exceeding analyst estimates for both revenue and earnings per share. Despite strong overall performance, particularly in Singapore, the stock fell over 8% in after-hours trading due to concerns about net income and margins in the Macao region. The company's CEO, Robert G. Goldstein, emphasized continued investment in both markets and returning capital to stockholders, though Macao's performance tempered investor enthusiasm.

feed.title: AllianceBernstein L P : AB Private Credit Investors Announces Strategic Rebrand of Fund & Manager Finance Platform
feed.summary: AllianceBernstein's NAV Lending team within AB Private Credit Investors (AB-PCI) has rebranded as AB-PCI Fund & Manager Finance (FMF). This strategic rebrand emphasizes the platform's ability to provide non-dilutive capital to both funds and private capital investment managers, leveraging AB-PCI's existing infrastructure. The FMF team aims to offer flexible, tailored financing solutions as private markets mature, ensuring more efficient diligence and decision-making for their sponsor relationships and investors.

feed.title: Fair Isaac: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Fair Isaac Corp. (FICO) reported strong fiscal first-quarter results, with profits of $158.4 million and adjusted earnings of $7.33 per share, significantly exceeding Wall Street's expectations of $6.95 per share. The company also surpassed revenue forecasts, posting $512 million against an anticipated $498.2 million. FICO projects full-year earnings of $38.17 per share and revenue of $2.35 billion.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial Reports Fiscal First Quarter of 2026 Results
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial (NYSE: RJF) has reported strong fiscal first-quarter results for 2026, with record net revenues of $3.74 billion, record client assets under administration of $1.77 trillion, and record Private Client Group assets in fee-based accounts of $1.04 trillion. The company achieved diluted earnings per share of $2.79, or an adjusted $2.86 per diluted share, and saw significant growth across its Private Client Group, Asset Management, and Bank segments, while Capital Markets revenues were down. The firm also announced the acquisition of Clark Capital Management Group and increased its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Jefferies Financial Group Has Lowered Expectations for WEC Energy Group (NYSE:WEC) Stock Price
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has decreased its price target for WEC Energy Group (NYSE:WEC) from $124.00 to $121.00, while maintaining a "hold" rating, suggesting a potential upside of approximately 9.9% from its current share price. The consensus among analysts remains a "Hold" with an average price target of $120.40. WEC Energy Group's shares are trading around $110.11, with the company providing FY2025 EPS guidance between $5.17 and $5.27.

feed.title: Assessing Teledyne Technologies (TDY) Valuation After Strong 2025 Results And Fresh 2026 Earnings Guidance
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies (TDY) recently released strong full-year 2025 earnings and new 2026 guidance, highlighting positive momentum from its defense, imaging, and commercial sectors. The company's stock has seen significant short-term and long-term gains, supported by a disciplined M&A strategy and a $400 million share buyback. While Simply Wall St's narrative suggests the stock is 8% undervalued at $665.77, a discounted cash flow model presents a lower fair value of $551.12, indicating potential optimism in current market pricing.

feed.title: Assessing WesBanco (WSBC) Valuation After Earnings, Integration Progress And Branch Closure Plans
feed.summary: WesBanco (WSBC) recently reported its Q4 and full year 2025 results, showing increased net interest income and fee growth, along with progress in its Premier Financial integration and plans for branch closures. The stock has seen a 17.64% return over 90 days, leading to investor interest in its current valuation. While one narrative suggests WesBanco is 5.9% undervalued with a fair value of $37.43, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model points to a greater undervaluation, with an estimated future cash flow value of $68.91 compared to its current price of $35.22.

feed.title: IBM beats Q4 estimates as generative AI business tops $12.5B
feed.summary: International Business Machines Corp (NYSE:IBM) surpassed Q4 revenue and profit expectations, driven by strong software sales and the growth of its generative AI business, which now exceeds $12.5 billion. The company reported $19.69 billion in revenue and an operating EPS of $4.52, both beating analyst forecasts. Following these positive results, IBM's shares rose 7.7% in aftermarket trading.

feed.title: Alteryx launches in-warehouse data prep tool for BigQuery
feed.summary: Alteryx has expanded its partnership with Google Cloud, introducing Live Query for BigQuery, a new feature allowing Alteryx workflows to run directly within BigQuery. This eliminates the need to move data, reducing egress costs, security risks, and enabling greater analytical scale. The collaboration also includes Alteryx One: Google Edition, a purpose-built version for Google Cloud customers available via the Google Cloud Marketplace.

feed.title: Lead auditor sues Cboe, alleges firing after flagging concerns, seeking leave
feed.summary: Jacqueline Craine, a former Lead Auditor at Cboe Global Markets, is suing the company, alleging she was fired in retaliation for flagging accounting irregularities and then denied medical leave. Craine claims that despite assurances from HR, her concerns were dismissed, and she was subsequently terminated shortly after requesting FMLA leave for a chest trauma. The case highlights the complex legal risks for HR teams when whistleblower complaints, internal investigations, performance reviews, and medical leave requests intersect.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks May Gain ‘First-Mover Advantage’ On Quantum Readiness: Analyst
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks is proactively addressing the future threat of quantum computing to cybersecurity, hosting a Quantum Safe Summit to raise industry awareness. TD Cowen analyst Shaul Eyal believes this early focus positions Palo Alto Networks for a "first-mover advantage" in the post-quantum transition. The company is already offering tools like a Quantum Readiness Dashboard and quantum-safe security capabilities for its firewall and SASE customers.

feed.title: Microsoft Continues to Spend Big on A.I. While Profit Jumps 60 Percent
feed.summary: Microsoft announced a significant increase in profit, up 60% to $38.5 billion, and revenue of $81.3 billion for its latest quarter, exceeding Wall Street expectations. The company continues to heavily invest in AI infrastructure, spending $37.5 billion on data centers, a 65% increase year-over-year. Despite these strong financial results, Microsoft's shares dropped over 5% in after-hours trading.

feed.title: Gartner predicts fewer than 20 companies will deploy humanoids at scale by 2028
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that by 2028, fewer than 20 companies will deploy humanoid robots at scale, primarily for supply chain and manufacturing use cases. Despite significant investment and media attention, the technology is considered immature and ill-suited for complex, unstructured environments. Gartner suggests that "polyfunctional" robots, designed for flexibility without human-like constraints, will be a superior solution for most companies seeking to maximize throughput per dollar invested.

feed.title: S&T Bancorp, Inc. Declares Dividend
feed.summary: S&T Bancorp, Inc. announced its board of directors approved a cash dividend of $0.36 per share. This represents a 5.88% increase from the previous year's dividend of $0.34 per share. The dividend is payable on February 26, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 12, 2026.

feed.title: Quest 401(k) Win Suit Faces Scrutiny at Third Circuit
feed.summary: A federal appeals court is scrutinizing a pretrial victory for Quest Diagnostics in a lawsuit alleging mismanagement of workers' 401(k) savings. Judges are questioning whether the company followed its own investment rules, potentially rekindling a proposed class-action suit from former employees. The case hinges on whether Quest's investment committee diligently managed and monitored the plan's offerings or if the pretrial victory was granted prematurely.
